mapping.a_element: bidirectional coupling between the real and virtual pendulum
mapping.b_element: coupling between the qubits and the cavity mode
critical coupling for the super-radiant transition

brief_justification: The only real overlap is generic: both feature a coupling parameter with a threshold separating two qualitative states. But the driving machinery differs — paper A is classical nonlinear coupled-oscillator synchronization (instantaneous bidirectional feedback locking two pendulums into phase), while paper B is a quantum many-body first-order super-radiant phase transition of a Dicke-type qubit-cavity Hamiltonian; there is no role-for-role correspondence between phase-locking dynamics and a collective quantum phase transition.
